summary: Smooth‑ish audience recording of an energetic 6/30/73 show, though the tape is muddy and high‑end stripped by noise reduction.
review: The performance shines – fans rave about the tight playing and a roaring start with The Promised Land – but the audience tape suffers from muddiness and a noticeable loss of top‑end clarity, likely due to aggressive noise reduction. While the sound remains listenable, it doesn’t match the quality of the following night’s recording.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (near‑perfect performance) while recording quality is described as smooth yet muddy and high‑end limited; the weighted average yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Muddy, slightly muffled sound with missing high frequencies – likely due to noise reduction or tape age – but still listenable and smoother than some other audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High energy from the opening of The Promised Land, maintaining a fun, roaring vibe throughout; overall set feels tight and lively.
